zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Burdon於1295年出生在英國越可爾約克時，她的父親Lord Kexby Brian Burdon25歲，母親Isabel de Meaux17歲。她於1328年1月29日與Sir Thomas Ughtred 1st Baron Ughtred結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1365年5月12日在英國越可爾斯卡波洛享年70歲。

nameMeanings.name: Burdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Polish: nickname for a troublemaker (see   Burda  ).

variants.name: Burden
meanings: (southern England, of Norman origin): from a diminutive of the ancient Germanic personal name Burdo, for which compare Burdett . Burdonus and other derivatives of the Gaulish personal name Burdo (‘mule’) are found in France, where it is rare and the similar French surname may derive instead from Old French bourdon in the sense ‘pilgrim's staff’ (compare Bourdon 1). The personal name, whether of ancient Germanic or Gaulish origin, does occur in medieval England, but rarely.

habitational name from any of various places called Burdon or Burden. Burden in Yorkshire and Great Burdon in County Durham are named with Old English burh ‘stronghold, fortified place’ + dūn ‘hill’; Burdon in Tyne and Wear is named with Old English b̄re ‘byre’ + denu ‘valley’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Burdon was born in 1295, in York, Yorkshire, England, her father, Lord Kexby Brian Burdon, was 25 and her mother, Isabel de Meaux, was 17. She married Sir Thomas Ughtred 1st Baron Ughtred on 29 January 1328. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 12 May 1365, in Scarborough, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Burdon est née en 1295, à York, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Lord Kexby Brian Burdon, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Isabel de Meaux, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Ughtred 1st Baron Ughtred le 29 janvier 1328. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 12 mai 1365, à Scarborough, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Burdon(이)가 1295년 영국 요르크실르 요르크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lord Kexby Brian Burdon(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel de Meaux(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1328년 1월 29일 Sir Thomas Ughtred 1st Baron Ughtred(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1365년 5월 12일 영국 요르크실르 스카버루그h에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.
